entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - Smart JMS Retry with Exception-Based Routing in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello @mitishah ,

Thank you for reading and sharing your thoughts!

To answer your first question: yes, I highly recommend this smart retry approach for production environments. When dealing with high message volumes, managing thread concurrency is crucial. This pattern ensures that worker nodes are not blocked by synchronous failures, keeping the tenant's overall performance stable.

Regarding common pitfalls and real-world lessons, the biggest trap is the overuse of the DLQ pattern. My strict approach is to implement JMS DLQs only if the business criticality of the integration is explicitly defined and truly justifies it.

Unfortunately, in the real world, this is rarely the case. Many companies and even integration architects fail to provide clear criticality classifications for their interfaces. What happens when this governance detail is missing? Developers defensively apply DLQs to all scenarios. This ultimately overloads the JMS broker, exhausts the tenant's retry mechanisms with non-critical messages, and creates a massive operational bottleneck.

So, my main design consideration is this: never implement a DLQ without a clear business requirement. Governance must drive the architecture, not the other way around.

Regards, Viana

entry.title: Component Colour Management with AVC and Material Variants in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) in SAP S/4HANA is a tool for managing complex configurable products. It's often positioned as the make-to-order or engineer-to-order sales configurator for S/4HANA, but it can also support different lightweight use cases. In this blog post, I will describe one manufacturing and logistics related use case: component colour management.

Use Case Introduction

Let’s introduce the use case with the classic bike example. Bikes are sold in many different colours, and the selected colour must be transferred from sales order item through BOM to relevant components. These components need the colour information so they can either be purchased in the correct colour or painted in-house. Not all components, like bike tires, are colour-relevant. Only colour-relevant components should be affected by the selected colour. The key challenge is logistics: different colours must be separated in inventory and in downstream logistics processes.

To put it as a business requirement:

  • Colour is only selected in the sales order item.
  • Only colour-relevant components inherit the colour.
  • Inventory must be separated by different colours.

Solution

The core idea is to model colour as an AVC characteristic on the finished product and use BOM class items to select the correct material variant for each colour-relevant component. Material variants are needed because colour must become logistics-relevant. It is possible to transfer the AVC colour characteristic to the generic component, but this has no impact on logistics processes. Alternatively, batch management could be used, but there is no standard automatic way to transfer AVC characteristics to batch classification.

The solution process flow is as follows:

  1. Colour is selected in the sales order item configuration.
  2. During BOM explosion, the BOM class item uses the colour value to determine the matching material variant.
  3. The selected coloured material variant is then used in downstream logistics processes such as procurement, production, inventory management, and picking.

Setup

The example below is based on S/4HANA Public Cloud, but the functionality should be the same in Private Cloud as well.

The first step is to define colour as a characteristic and assign it to an AVC class (type 300). In my example, the colour is defined with RAL codes, so the characteristic is a 4-digit numeric value.

Important note: to enable material variant selection through class items, the class must be material-specific. In my example, RM440 is the material number for the component. This means that a class must be created for each colour-relevant component. For a productive implementation, it probably makes sense to automate this.

Class and characteristic

Material variants must be created for all possible colour variants, with material-specific class assigned and colour value defined. Again, for a productive implementation, automation is recommended. Generic configurable “parent” component (here RM440) is not strictly mandatory, but makes managing the variants easier.

Material variantsExample of variant

To enable material variant selection based on the colour characteristic, a class item must be added to the BOM.

BOM with class item

The finished product must be configurable (KMAT) and have a class assigned with the colour characteristic. It’s not necessary to assign the material-specific class to the finished product, as long as the same colour characteristic is available there. In the example, I used a generic colour class, which also enables selection of multiple colour-relevant components based on one characteristic value in the sales order item.

Finished product with class assignment

Configuration profile must also exist for the finished product.

Finished product configuration profile

Demo

That’s the setup: no AVC dependencies are needed. Below is a short demo video showing how it works in simulation mode.

Conclusion

This setup provides a clean-core-friendly solution for component colour management where colour must be logistics-relevant. The main trade-off is material master complexity: using material variants leads to a high number of materials, making material master maintenance more challenging. For that reason, automation and proper master data management are important for productive use.

entry.title: Re: SAF-T Portugal - Incorrect Opening Balance calculatio...
entry.content:
Hi 6-Mar,

Short answer: The SAF-T opening balance is derived from the balance carryforward, so in-period adjustment journals can never realign it. The likely root cause is a non-standard mid-year load: the Jan–March balances were brought in on manually-created transfer accounts instead of the auto-reversing Historical Balance object, which doubled the opening. Fix the duplicate at its source, make the opening equal the true carryforward, then regenerate the file — and because this is a productive migrated ledger, treat it as a controlled correction, not a self-service journal.

Why the SAF-T opening ignores your adjustment postings

This is expected behavior, not a bug. SAF-T PT Accounting (report category PT_SAFT_ACCOUNTING) is generated in the Run Statutory Reports app, and the GeneralLedger section reports OpeningDebit/CreditBalance from the account's balance carryforward — not by re-summing your period documents — Reference: Generating the SAF-T Accounting File.

A journal posted in 2025 is a period movement (turnover): it affects the closing balance but never the reported opening. So the file keeps the carried-forward figure as opening, while your compensating entries sit in the movements, and Opening + Debit − Credit no longer ties to the book balance you corrected — Reference: Balance Carryforward.

Likely cause of the gap: the SAF-T value (56,863.33) matches the correct 31/12/2024 close, so it is most likely the correct opening; the doubled 113,726.66 is on the book side, from a duplicated migration/transfer posting that your adjustments then masked. (Deduction from your figures — 113,726.66 = 2 × 56,863.33 — confirm with the validation report below.)

Has anyone faced this after an S/4HANA migration?

Yes — and it usually comes from how the mid-year balances were loaded. For an in-year go-live, SAP's approach is one migration key date: balance-sheet accounts as the closing balance at the key date, P&L accounts as current-year movements from day one. Prior-year and prior-month detail (Jan–March) is meant to be loaded with the FI – Historical Balance object, which auto-reverses and is for reporting only — Reference: SAP KBA 2943035 (Migration of historical balances). When teams instead create their own transfer accounts and post those prior-period balances manually, the postings don't reverse, the opening is counted twice, and the carryforward that feeds SAF-T is distorted.

How mid-year migration should establish the opening (so you fix the right layer)

Operational balance: the FI – G/L account balance object posts only balances (no documents) to the migration key date, offset to an initial clearing account — Reference: FI – G/L account balance and open/line item.

Reporting history (Jan–March): the Historical Balance object loads it into already-closed periods and auto-creates the reversal, so it never disturbs the go-live balance or the carryforward.

Constraint: if the opening sits on hand-posted transfer accounts (not the Historical Balance object), those entries are real and non-reversing — that is where the duplicate lives.

Recommended correction approach (Public Cloud)

Fix the source, not the symptom.

  • Diagnose first. In Run Statutory Reports, before generating, run the General Ledger Account Validation Report — it lists each account with balance vs. expected balance and a semaphore, pinpointing 4310000001 and the exact gap that fails AT validation — Reference: Generating the SAF-T Accounting File.
  • Locate the duplicate. In Display G/L Account Balances, enter the account, company code and the cutover fiscal year, and see whether the extra 56,863.33 is in the period-0 carryforward, in the go-live migration document, or on the manually-created Jan–March transfer accounts.
  • Reverse the compensating adjustments (Manage Journal Entries) and remove the duplicated transfer posting at its origin. Net effect on the correct closing balance is zero.
  • If the opening itself must change: it only changes when you post into the previous fiscal year — the carryforward then updates automatically, or re-run it via Schedule General Ledger Jobs — Reference: Balance Carryforward. A current-year journal will not do it.
  • Regenerate and resubmit PT_SAFT_ACCOUNTING; if already submitted, produce the fix via a correction phase (a full corrected file is generated), not by editing the old run.

Important:

  • The productive load cannot be re-run or reset. The Reset Transaction Data job works only in the quality system; in production, corrections are manual journals against the migration offset account — Reference: Reset Transaction data 
  • Open an SAP incident (FI-LOC-SAF-PT / Document and Reporting Compliance) before correcting a productive migrated ledger to ensure the proper approach based on the real system data is taken

Summary

The SAF-T opening comes from the carryforward and can't be fixed with in-period journals. The most likely cause is that the Jan–March balances were hand-posted to created transfer accounts instead of the auto-reversing Historical Balance object, doubling the opening. Run the General Ledger Account Validation Report to confirm the layer, reverse the compensating adjustments and remove the duplicated posting at its origin (keeping the correct closing intact); only a prior-year posting plus a carryforward re-run actually moves the opening. Since the load can't be reset in production and prior periods may be closed, raise an SAP incident before touching production, then regenerate PT_SAFT_ACCOUNTING via a correction phase.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: SSL in HANA Cockpit
entry.content:
SSL Configuration in SAP HANA Cockpit.

SAP HANA Cockpit is a primary administration and recommended tool to manage SAP HANA databases. It provides some additional features and steps that are not available in HANA Studio.

 

Purpose.
This blog describes the steps to configure SSL for the SAP HANA Cockpit.

Overview: SSL Configuration for SAP HANA Cockpit.

Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) configuration for SAP HANA Cockpit is essential to ensure secure and encrypted communication between users (browsers) and the HANA Cockpit system.

 

Key Purpose.

Protect sensitive data from unauthorized access.
Ensure secure login and data transmission.


How It Works.

SSL uses digital certificates to encrypt communication.
The SAP HANA Cockpit is configured to use HTTPS instead of HTTP.
Certificates are issued by a trusted Certificate Authority (CA) or created internally if you have SSO Server in your environment.

 

High-Level Steps.

HANA Cockpit Should be installed in your environment.
Technical Communication User should be created.
SYSTEM and TENANT DB must be added in Cockpit.
Generate SSL certificate from OS level with sidadm user.
Sign certificate with your CA authority or you can sign self if SSO Server in your environment.
Import certificate into HANA XSA keystore.
Configure HTTPS settings in HANA Cockpit.
Restart Cockpit services to activate SSL.
Access Cockpit using https://:

Benefits.
Secure browser access.

Prerequisites.

HANA Cockpit Should be installed in your environment.
Sign certificate with your CA authority.
SSO Server installed (Optional)

Team Requirements.

Basis Consultant will install Cockpit and Configure SSL.

Certificate Authority  will Sign Certificate. 

Now We are going to Configure SSL in HANA Cockpit.

Step 1

Launch HANA Cockpit. We can see in Green Color Not secure coming in HANA Cockpit URL.

Step 2

Run env for checking the sec dir path

Go to this path ---> /usr/sap/D1C/HDB00/ and take backup of sec dir.

Step 3

Backup has been taken with sec_bkp_21_05_2021 name

 

Step 4

Now generate the pse and csr name. I am generating here with this name domain.pse and domain.csr

Now run below command.


sapgenpse gen_pse -p domain.pse -.r domain.csr -k "GN-dNSName:FQDN.com" -k "GN-dNSName:hostname" "CN=FQDN.com, OU=IT,

Step 5

Now check ls –ltr and see domanin.pse and domain.csr file generated.  in /usr/sap/dic/HDB00/hostname/sec

Step 6

Now open domain.csr file for checking the certificate.  Copy this Certificate and Sign it from your CA Authority or SSO Server.

We have our SSO Server for signing the certificate.

I am login in my SSO Server.

Now login in SSO Server.

Step 7

Go to Certificate Management--> Sign certificate requests and paste that certificate.

Click on Show Certificate Request.

Step 8

Click on Sign Certificate and after Sign copy the Certificate

Step 9

 

Step 10

Now create one file with domain.p7b and paste Signed certificate in this file.

/usr/sap/SID/HDB00/Hostname/sec

Step 11

I pasted Singed Certificated in domain.p7b file.

 

Step 12

Now import the certificate with below command.

sapgenpse import_own_cert -p domain.pse -c domain.p7b

Certificated imported Successfully.

Step 13

Now we need to set certificate with domain. What domain we have run xs domains command for checking the domain name. We have only one domain.

 

Before set the certificate run this command for checking the certificate status. xs domain-certificates

We have no certificate now.

 

Step 14

Now run this for set the certificate    xs set-certificate host --pse domain.pse

 

Step 15

Certificate done now restart the XSA from XSA restart command.

Step 16

Now check again XSA Domain Certificate   xs domain-certificates we can see certificate with signed.

Now check the https URL from xs version command.

You can see hana-cockpit URL showing https://hostname:51026

Step 17

Now run this command XSA diagnose for checking the issue in system.

Now issue coming all is ok.

Step 18

Now run this command for open the Cockpit with ----> https://hostname:51026

 

HANA Cockpit SSL has been completed.

 

Conclusion

SSL in SAP HANA Cockpit makes the system safe by protecting data between the user and the system.
It allows access using HTTPS, which improves security and reliability.

entry.title: Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): Enablement of Sales Kits
entry.content:
• Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): Enablement of Sales Kits
  • Overview
  • Capability and Benefit
  • Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • More Information/ Additional links
  • SAP Help
  • Scope Items
  • Blogs and FAQs
  • Notes - Restrictions and General Information

Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): Enablement of Sales Kits

Overview

With SAP Cloud ERP 2602 ​Sales kit (Sales BOM) processing can now be enabled in advanced intercompany sales. This feature increase operational efficiency and reduce complexity by using predefined products that consist of several components. This feature reduces manual effort and increases automation by using Monitor Value Chain to automatically post the intercompany purchase document and the intercompany sales order for inventory-controlled goods, and to monitor further and orchestrate the process.

The following graphic illustrates the business process:

Graphic: Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits

Capability and Benefit

This capability lets a selling organization offer a sales kit while an affiliated delivering company fulfills it. A kit contains both inventory-controlled items and at least one non-inventory-controlled item; intercompany billing and stock movements apply only to the inventory-controlled components. The process supports multiple kit types already available in sell-from-stock, ranging from main-item level processing to bracket-style kits where sub-items carry pricing, inventory control, and delivery. When the delivering plant’s company code differs from the selling company’s, the advanced intercompany flow is triggered: the customer-facing sales order holds the full kit, while the intercompany PO, SO, and invoices include only the stock-managed sub-items. The outbound delivery shows the full kit, with goods movements on inventory-relevant sub-items, and the Value Chain Monitoring app automatically creates and orchestrates intercompany documents with end-to-end document flow visibility.

This enables multinational sales of complete kits without adding operational complexity, ensuring costs and revenues are correctly posted between affiliates for the inventory-managed components. Predefined multi-component products reduce manual steps and errors, while automation via Value Chain Monitoring minimizes effort and shortens cycle times. Supporting multiple kit types provides flexibility to fit varied pricing and fulfillment patterns, helping standardize cross-company operations and improve transparency. Overall, it boosts revenue opportunities with kits, streamlines intercompany processing, and enhances financial accuracy across delivering and selling entities.

Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales

The following table gives you an overview of the most important differences between the available ways of processing for items:

  • Processing on Main Item Level (ERLA)
  • Processing on Subitem Level (LUMF)
  • Processing on Both Main Item and Subitem Level: Pricing on Main Item Level (CPFH)
  • Processing on Both Main Item and Subitem Level: Pricing on Subitem Level (DISH)

Table: Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales

For CPFH and DISH the profitability segment is propagated (that is, copied, along with other related account assignment fields) from the main item to the subitems.

This video explains the concept:

 

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

More Information / Additional links

SAP Help

  • Advanced Intercompany Sales

  • Sales Kits in Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • Sales Kits in Sales Documents
  • Monitor Value Chains

Scope Items

Best Practices Explorer (including process flow, test-script and set-up instructions):

  • 5D2 - Advanced Intercompany Sales

Blogs and FAQs

  • Advanced Intercompany Sales & Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer
  • Advanced Intercompany Sales - Insights into Value Chain
  • Advanced Intercompany Sales MTO and Variant Configuration (7DM) in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
  • How Transit Plants work in the Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • FAQ new Advanced Intercompany Processes (IC-Sales and IC-Stock Transfer) Procurement
  • Intercompany integration scenarios FAQ in Cloud ERP, Public Edition

 Notes - Restrictions and General Information

  • 3192584 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Restrictions and General Information for Advanced Intercompany Sales
  • 3192546 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Restrictions and General Information for Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer
  • 3100892 - Group Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • 3093830 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Release Restriction Note for Group Valuation

entry.title: SAP TM Basic : handling subsequent freight charges without Advanced TM insert API
entry.content:
Hi all,

On Basic TM (S/4HANA), the actual freight charges come from an external invoice management system, not from within SAP TM. SAP TM is essentially used to bring the freight cost into Finance.

For the first actual invoice, we use standard Reverse and Repost (credit memo cancels the planned FSD, new FSD created with actual charges). Works fine on Basic TM.

The problem is subsequent invoices for the same shipment (e.g. a later detention charge after the first invoice is already posted and paid). Adding only the delta via the charge element insert API seems to need Advanced TM. The Basic TM full-image API would reverse and repost the whole invoice, including the already paid part, which we want to avoid.

Alternative idea: create a separate Freight Order for the delta charges only, linked to the same shipment reference, with its own FSD and posting.

  • Is this a supported pattern on Basic TM?
  • Best way to link the two Freight Orders?
  • Any Finance issue (accruals, GR/IR, cost object) with two settlement chains for one shipment, assuming same account assignment?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to migrate Revision Level using the Inspection Pl...
entry.content:
Hello @16175219 

Thank you for your question.

 

Based on your screenshot, the Revision Level field is available during manual inspection plan creation, but this does not automatically mean that the same field is supported by the stranded migration object.

Question 1: Is the Revision Level supported by the standard Inspection plan migration object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Answer: If the field is not available in the delivered QM Inspection plan migration template, then it is not supported by that standard migration object.

See the following references for more insights

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2683477?

 

Question 2: If yes, where should it be maintained in the migration template?

Answer: There is no supported place to maintain it if the delivered template has no Revision Level field. Migrate Your Data uses predefined migration objects and their delivered template structures, adding a missing field to the XML template is not the right approach.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 3: If not, what is the recommended approach to migrate Inspection Plans with Revision Levels?

Answer: Migrate the supported inspection plan data first, such as header, operations, and characteristics, using QM-Inspection Plan. This migration object is recorded for inspection data. After you have completed the migration, maintain the revision level manually in Manage Inspection Plans, where inspection plans can be created and managed.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/cbb6d3e190d84558b7b3ddb90495801c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d1e58be39d884a0dbf75a7526a9acbf4/a231727866504392825de9bbf33f0ce2.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 4: Has anyone successfully implemented a workaround or alternative process for this requirements?

Answer: Ensure you avoid            workarounds such as changing the migration template structure or using unreleased objects. If mass maintenance is required, you should check the Inspection Plan API metadata in your system. It records the Inspection Plan API as supporting create, read, and update operations for inspection plan data, but you must verify whether the Revision Level field is exposed in your release. If the field is not available in the migration object or the released API, I suggest you raise a case or submit an improvement request.

See the following references for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/cbb6d3e190d84558b7b3ddb90495801c.html?locale=en-US

 

 

In Migrate Your Data, you should select migration object QM Inspection plan and download the latest template.

 

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Budget Check Exception（Delivery date is empty） When Adjusting Service Product PR Quantity
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have enabled budget availability check for internal orders at the PR creation stage. When adjusting the quantity of service product PRs, the end dates of service items  did not be updated, triggering the following warning messages. If you ignore the warning and proceed to save, such PRs will not occupy the internal order budget. And the delivery date of PR is empty in Table EBAN-LFDAT.

Material SRV_01060 not listed in plant SH00 (error 4, see long text)

Message no. 06633

Diagnosis

Material SRV_01060 is not listed in plant SH00 for the specified delivery date (or procurement period).

How could we solve this issue? 

Thanks for your support.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Request for Alternative SAP App for Invoice-wise Coll...
entry.content:
Good day @tufanbiswas 

Thank you for your question.

 

In Public cloud, if your requirement is to post an incoming customer payment and clear it invoice wise, the approach is still Post Incoming Payments and Clear Incoming Payments.

For manual AR collection posting and clearing, i would not suggest replacing both. Make use of Post Incoming Payments to post and clear a single incoming payment in one step. For manual clearing, use Clear Incoming Payments to clear your receivable payment.

Here are references for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/fc628a542f2b0165e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/340f87546ccd9c62e10000000a445394.html?locale=en-US

 

Clearing is not a display activity, but rather It creates or changes accounting clearing information. So, a role with only read access will correctly lead to an authorization error. Use Maintain Business Roles to assign business catalogs and maintain areas such as value help, read, and write access restrictions.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/8980ad05330b4585ab96a8e09cef4688.html?locale=en-US

 

If the payment information comes from the bank, i suggest you use bank statement processing instead.  You can import electronic payment files with Manage Incoming Payments Files. If you cannot clear an item automatically, you can be handle it in Reprocess Bank Statement Items or Clear Incoming Payments. Cash Applications can also propose matches between incoming bank statement items and open receivables. it can also automatically run them.

Here are references for more guiding details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/a1597b757dfd404283a050ab12585e5a.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/a21af993466648b994144cdd15ab1a39.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CASH_APPLICATION/76882b4bdf70467da9c68359d55b62d9/6da671563df64cf983947dbca148a163.html?locale=en-US

 

Here is a quick guide:

In IAM Information System, you can check which business catalogs are required. You can display information about business roles, IAM, business catalogs, business users and restrictions are recorded.

In Maintain Business Roles, you can add the required catalogs to the role used by the collection user.

Maintain write access for the specific company code and accounting restrictions. You can assign the role to the user and retest the clearing.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/82d17cfdb0f3464b9735e4ded705f71f.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to set BRIM FICA ODI step as Technically Failed i...
entry.content:
Hi,

The BAdI CRM_ISX_ERP_CONTRACT is meant for enrichment of data that is being sent from the Subscription Contract to the FICA Provider Contract. I do not think the processing should be blocked in the BAdI implementation.

If you need to do this derivation from a different system during the distribution, then I would suggest creating a custom ODI step and class which inherits from the standard distribution class (For example, if you are using step PCEA then it would be CL_CRM_ISX_ORDER_MD_ERP_MCC). In this custom class then depending on your scenario you can set the status to 'Technically Failed' or 'Failed'.

Thanks,
Vinay

entry.title: Intercompany STO - Use Sales Price (ESP2) as Purchase Price in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We are implementing an Intercompany Stock Transfer scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Scenario

Delivering Plant: 1050 (Company Code 1010, Germany)
Receiving Store: 3940 (Company Code 3910, Austria)

Our requirement is to determine the purchase price of the intercompany stock transfer from the sales price condition (ESP2).

Currently, the standard pricing procedure uses condition type PPR0, which determines the price via Purchasing Info Records.

We created our own condition type ZVPV as a copy of PPR0. However, in the Partner Demo system the access sequence cannot be modified because it is restricted.

Our questions are:

Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to determine the intercompany purchase price directly from the sales condition ESP2?
Is maintaining Purchasing Info Records the SAP standard approach, or is there a recommended alternative?
Is the restriction of changing Access Sequences only a limitation of the Partner Demo system, or does the same restriction exist in productive Public Cloud systems?
What is the SAP Best Practice for this scenario?

We have attached screenshots showing:

Purchase Order Pricing Analysis
Pricing Procedure
Sales Order Pricing (ESP2 determined successfully)

Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards,
Mustafa Kocabeyoglu

entry.title: Re: sapui5 - updating the bootstrap to release 1.149 / 1.150
entry.content:
SAPUI5 1.149 and also 1.150 are cloud-only deliveries. So you won't get them for any ABAP onPremise system.

See also SAP Note 2217489.

The notification you got is only about SAPUI5 patches that are planned to be deleted on the SAPUI5 CDN, which is the cloud-channel for consuming SAPUI5. So, this should have actually not impact on your onPremise system - at least not for minor versions. But you must only check that the latest patch on your onPremise system is still available on CDN in case you are using any SAPUI5 application on premise with SAP Work Zone.

entry.title: Force password change for Dialog user IDs on next login due to security policy change
entry.content:
Due to a change in Security policy, we have to force all the dialog user IDs to change the password in the next login. Is there any option available to force password change for Dialog user IDs on next login without a system restart?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Force password change for Dialog user IDs on next log...
entry.content:
As the KBA 3460608 mentions:

"Existing passwords can be forced to change to the new rules on next login by setting the parameter login/password_compliance_to_current_policy = 1 (this still does not affect System and Service users, only Dialog and Communication)"

 

Please also read the KBA 3495035:

  • Refer to the documentation of the parameter login/password_compliance_to_current_policy, which can be accessed in transaction code RZ11.
  • This parameter controls whether the system checks if the password used for a password-based logon fulfills the current password rules and, if necessary, prompts the user to change their password.
  • If the parameter is set to 1, users of type DIALOG and COMMUNICATION, that do not fit the new rules defined by password/logon parameters, will be required to change their password on next password-based logon. 
  • If the parameter is set to 0, DIALOG and COMMUNICATION will only have to change the password once it expires, which is controlled by parameter login/password_expiration_time.     
  • For COMMUNICATION user, Although the system checks whether the password has expired or is initial, the implementation of the requirement to change the password, which exists in principle, depends on the logon method (interactive or non-interactive). 
  • Users of types 'SERVICE' and 'SYSTEM' are excluded in principle from the requirement to change passwords and are therefore not affected by this parameter.

 

 

The login/password_compliance_to_current_policy is a 'Dynamic Parameter', therefore does not require a system restart.

entry.title: Re: CJ88 getting error- Not possible to post down payment...
entry.content:
First, ensure that the sum of down payments and down payment clearings equals zero in all depreciation areas. If this condition is not met, error AA 571 will occur.

If down payments and their clearings are posted in a prior fiscal year (and are therefore not considered in the current year's settlement), it is necessary to refer to SAP Note 2725359 - Investment measure settlement: Settlement of sender costs from previous years.

Also the following KBA can be helpful too:

3470077 - Error AA571 issued during settlement to asset

entry.title: Fixing Sort, Group & Reset in Fiori Elements with CAP CDS What's Not Standard and What's Needed
entry.content:
The Problem

In a SAP CAP Fiori Elements List Report or Object Page application, you might find that Sort, Group, and Reset personalization features do not work out of the box, especially when your entity uses navigation properties (associations) as display columns.

Symptoms

  • Clicking Sort on a column throws a backend error or does nothing
  • Group dialog shows columns that cannot be grouped, causing OData errors
  • Reset or Restore button does not work, or personalization state is lost on page refresh
  • Search works but sort and group personalization is broken

Example Scenario

Consider an entity that displays details from an associated entity:

entity OrderItems {
  key order   : Association to Orders;
  key product : Association to Products;
}

Your @ui.LineItem annotation displays navigation properties:

annotate MyService.OrderItems with @(UI: {
  LineItem: [
    { Value: product_ID },          // flat key - works fine
    { Value: product.name },        // navigation property - breaks sort
    { Value: product.category },    // navigation property - breaks sort
    { Value: order.customerName }   // navigation property - breaks sort
  ]
});

Fiori Elements will show all these columns in the Sort and Group personalization dialog. But the OData backend cannot sort on navigation properties like product.name. Attempting to do so results in a runtime error.

What is Standard vs. What You Need to Add

Standard (works out of the box)

Feature Works For

SortingFlat or direct properties of the entity (e.g., product_ID, order_ID)
GroupingFlat or direct properties
Table rendering@ui.LineItem, @ui.PresentationVariant annotations
Search@CDS.search annotation on the entity

NOT Standard - You Must Configure These

The following three fixes are required when your table displays navigation properties.

Fix 1: SortRestrictions for Navigation Properties

When your @ui.LineItem displays columns from associations, Fiori Elements will offer these in the Sort and Group dialog by default. But the OData backend cannot sort on navigation properties.

The Fix

Add Capabilities.SortRestrictions.NonSortableProperties to exclude navigation properties from sort and group:

annotate MyService.OrderItems with @(
  Capabilities: {
    SortRestrictions: {
      NonSortableProperties: [
        product_ID,
        product.name,
        product.category,
        product.description,
        order.customerName,
        order.status
      ]
    }
  }
);

What This Does

  • Tells Fiori Elements not to offer these columns in the Sort or Group personalization dialog
  • Prevents runtime OData errors when users try to sort
  • The columns still display in the table. They just cannot be sorted or grouped.

When to Use

  • Any time your @ui.LineItem references a navigation property (e.g., product.name, order.type.description)
  • Deep navigation paths like order.statusRef.name

Fix 2: data-sap-ui-flexibility-services for Personalization Persistence

By default, Fiori Elements personalization (sort, group, filter variants) requires a flexibility service to store the user's settings. Without it, the Reset button may not work and personalization state is lost on refresh.

The Fix

Add the following attributes to your app's index.html bootstrap script:

<script
    id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
    src="https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-core.js"
    crossorigin="anonymous"
    data-sap-ui-theme="sap_horizon"
    data-sap-ui-resourceroots='{
        "com.mycompany.myapp": "./"
    }'
    data-sap-ui-oninit="module:sap/ui/core/ComponentSupport"
    data-sap-ui-compatVersion="edge"
    data-sap-ui-async="true"
    data-sap-ui-frameOptions="trusted"
    data-sap-ui-flexibility-services='[{"connector": "SessionStorageConnector"}]'
></script>

Key Attributes

Attribute Purpose

crossorigin="anonymous"Required for proper CORS handling of the UI5 library resources
data-sap-ui-flexibility-servicesConfigures where personalization state is stored
SessionStorageConnectorStores personalization in browser session storage (development or standalone)

Production vs. Development

  • Development or Standalone: Use SessionStorageConnector. It stores personalization in browser session storage.
  • Production on BTP Launchpad: The SAPUI5 Flexibility Service handles this automatically via the Launchpad shell. You typically do not need this attribute when deployed to a managed Launchpad.
  • Standalone production apps: Use SessionStorageConnector or configure a persistence backend.

Fix 3: @ui.HiddenFilter for Non-Filterable Fields

If your entity exposes fields via associations that should not appear in the filter bar's "Adapt Filters" dialog:

annotate MyService.Products with {
  description  @UI.HiddenFilter;
  category     @UI.HiddenFilter;
  manufacturer @UI.HiddenFilter;
  weight       @UI.HiddenFilter;
}

What This Does

  • Hides these fields from the "Adapt Filters" dialog in the List Report
  • Prevents users from trying to filter on fields that may not be efficiently queryable
  • The fields still display in tables. They are just not offered as filter options.

Complete Example

Here is a complete annotation setup for an entity with navigation properties:

// Entity definition with associations
entity OrderItems {
  key order   : Association to Orders;
  key product : Association to Products;
      quantity : Integer;
}

// Annotations
annotate MyService.OrderItems with @(
  // Prevent sort and group on navigation properties
  Capabilities: {
    SortRestrictions: {
      NonSortableProperties: [
        product_ID,
        product.name,
        product.category,
        product.description,
        order.customerName
      ]
    }
  },
  UI: {
    PresentationVariant: { Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem'] },
    LineItem: [
      { Value: product_ID,          ![@UI.Importance]: #High },
      { Value: product.name,        ![@UI.Importance]: #High },
      { Value: product.category,    ![@UI.Importance]: #Medium },
      { Value: order.customerName,  ![@UI.Importance]: #Medium },
      { Value: quantity }
    ]
  }
);

// Hide non-filterable fields from the Adapt Filters dialog
annotate MyService.Products with {
  description  @UI.HiddenFilter;
  category     @UI.HiddenFilter;
  manufacturer @UI.HiddenFilter;
}

Summary

Feature Standard? What to Add Where

Sort on flat propertiesYesNothingN/A
Sort on navigation propertiesNo, failsSortRestrictions.NonSortablePropertiesCDS annotations
Group on flat propertiesYesNothingN/A
Group on navigation propertiesNo, failsSortRestrictions.NonSortablePropertiesCDS annotations
Reset personalizationMay not workdata-sap-ui-flexibility-servicesindex.html
Personalization persistenceLost on refreshSessionStorageConnectorindex.html
Hide fields from filter dialogShows all@ui.HiddenFilterCDS annotations
CORS for UI5 resourcesMay warncrossorigin="anonymous"index.html

Key Takeaway

Fiori Elements is powerful but assumes your OData entity is flat. When you use CAP associations and display navigation properties in tables, you must explicitly tell the framework which columns are non-sortable and non-groupable. Without this, the personalization dialogs offer options that crash at runtime.

The three things to always check:

  1. SortRestrictions for every navigation property in your @ui.LineItem
  2. flexibility-services in your index.html for personalization to work
  3. @ui.HiddenFilter for fields that should not be in the filter dialog

References

  • CAP CDS Annotations: Capabilities
  • Fiori Elements: Configuring Tables
  • SAPUI5 Flexibility Services

entry.title: Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) deletion
entry.content:
Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) deletion  

With the release of SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS1, SAP delivers deletion report for Object-Based Authorization (OBA) framework for Project System. It helps administrators efficiently remove OBA-related access controls from existing projects and their subordinate objects. This tool offers a streamlined way to reset or clean up authorization assignments—particularly useful during restructuring, system cleanup, or when transitioning back to standard PFCG role-based authorization. 

For More details on OBA, refer to blog Introducing Object-Based Authorization (OBA) in SA... - SAP Community

OBA functionality provides a more granular and intuitive way to manage user access for specific project objects across selected transactions and use cases. OBA enhances security, improves control, and aligns authorization management with real-world project structures. 

Displaying and Removing Object-Based Authorizations (OBA) from a Project in SAP S/4HANA 

When a project is enabled for Object-Based Authorization (OBA), there may be scenarios where a customer wants to display  the OBA controls to get a complete overview of OBAs assigned within a Project or completely remove OBA controls from a project—for example, to simplify access management or revert to standard role-based authorizations. SAP provides a dedicated report to support this requirement. 

How to Display existing OBA Controls 

To display all OBA-related entries for a given project, execute the transaction CNOBADEL or report CNOBA_DELETE. 

Enter the Project or network and select Display button to display all existing OBA authorizations for the Project/Network. This report will display the OBA for the selected Object and for all the subordinate objects as shown below:- 

The above report can also be adjusted and exported via ALV functions. 

How to Remove OBA Controls 

To delete all OBA-related entries for a given project, execute the transaction CNOBADEL or report CNOBA_DELETE. 

This report clears all object-based authorization records from the relevant tables. After successful execution, the project will no longer be subject to OBA-driven restrictions. 

Using the “Keep Admin OBA” Option During OBA Deletion 

When executing the Deletion transaction/report, SAP provides an additional option—“Keep Admin OBA”—to help customers retain critical administrative access while removing other object-based authorizations. 

If the checkbox Keep Admin OBA is selected before triggering the deletion process, the system will remove all OBA records for every object under the project definition except those with the authorization type Admin (‘3’). 

This means: 

  • All non-admin OBA entries are deleted. 
  • Only OBA records granting administrative access are preserved across all project objects. 

This option is particularly useful when customers want to simplify or reset object-based authorizations while still ensuring that project administrators maintain full access. 

Post-Deletion Access Behavior 

Once OBA entries are completely removed: 

  • The project will rely exclusively on PFCG role-based authorizations for user access control. 
  • In transaction CJ20N, users will be able to view the project data without any OBA limitations. 
  • The Object-Based Authorization tab will no longer appear in the project’s detail screen, confirming that OBA controls have been fully removed. 

If required, we can reactivate OBA via running the report RPSOBA_MIGRATE.

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 2 — Roll & Move
entry.content:
Week 2 Submission : 

  1. Your srv/game-service.js handler implementations (paste as a JS code block)

    File text :
    (
    const cds = require('@sap/cds')

    module.exports = class GameService extends cds.ApplicationService {
    async init() {

    const { Players, BoardSquares } = this.entities

    // ── startTurn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Waiting] → CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Playing → CAP updates status after this returns
    // No business logic needed — CAP's @flow.status handles the transition.
    this.on('startTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // Intentionally empty: the @from/@to annotations do all the work.
    })

    // ── rollDice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Playing] → CAP validates status before this runs
    // @TO: #Moving → CAP updates status after this returns
    this.on('rollDice', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
    if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

    const roll = Math.ceil(Math.random() * 6)
    let newPosition = player.position + roll

    // Can't overshoot square 100 — must land exactly
    if (newPosition > 100) {
    await UPDATE(Players).set({ lastRoll: roll }).where({ ID: player.ID })
    const needed = 100 - player.position
    return req.reject(409, `Need exactly ${needed} to finish. Rolled ${roll}. No move.`)
    }

    // Resolve snake or ladder on the new square
    const square = await SELECT.one.from(BoardSquares).where({ square: newPosition })
    let finalPosition = newPosition
    let event = null

    if (square?.ladderTo) {
    finalPosition = square.ladderTo
    event = { type: 'ladder', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
    } else if (square?.snakeTo) {
    finalPosition = square.snakeTo
    event = square.isDoubleHead
    ? { type: 'doubleSnake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
    : { type: 'snake', 'from': newPosition, 'to': finalPosition }
    }

    // Persist position and last roll
    await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ position: finalPosition, lastRoll: roll })
    .where({ ID: player.ID })

    // Emit domain event so subscribers (Week 3) can react
    if (event) await this.emit('BoardEvent', { playerID: player.ID, ...event })

    // Use req.reply() — not return — to pin the response value before CAP's
    // @flow.status after-handler runs. A plain `return` gets cleared by the
    // flow machinery, producing HTTP 204 instead of the expected 200 + body.
    req.reply({ roll, position: finalPosition, event: event?.type ?? 'normal' })
    })

    // ── confirmMove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving] → normal end of a turn, hands off to next player (Week 3)
    // @TO: #Waiting → player waits for their next turn
    this.on('confirmMove', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // Week 3 will add turn rotation here.
    // For now: just let CAP apply @TO: #Waiting
    })

    // ── blockPlayer ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving] → called when player lands on a double-headed snake
    // @TO: #Blocked → player skips their next turn
    this.on('blockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // Business logic: the position was already updated in rollDice.
    // This action just transitions state — CAP handles the @TO: #Blocked update.
    req.info('Player is blocked and will skip their next turn.')
    })

    // ── unblockPlayer ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Blocked] → called at the start of a blocked player's next turn
    // @TO: $flow.previous → CAP restores the status to whatever it was before #Blocked
    this.on('unblockPlayer', 'Players', async (req) => {
    // CAP's $flow.previous does the work — no code needed here.
    // The player's status returns to #Waiting automatically.
    })

    // ── winGame ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    // @from: [#Moving] → called when player.position === 100
    // @TO: #Finished → player has won
    this.on('winGame', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
    if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

    // Mark the session as finished with this player as winner
    await UPDATE('snakeladder.GameSessions')
    .set({
    finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    winner_ID: player.ID
    })
    .where({ ID: player.session_ID })

    await this.emit('GameWon', { playerID: player.ID, sessionID: player.session_ID })
    })

    // ── Stretch Goal: skipTurn ────────────────────────────────────────────
    this.on('skipTurn', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const player = await SELECT.one.from(Players).where({ ID: req.params[0].ID })
    if (!player) return req.error(404, 'Player not found')

    await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ turnsBlocked: (player.turnsBlocked || 0) + 1 })
    .where({ ID: player.ID })
    })

    // ── CHEAT CODE: Teleport player for testing ───────────────────────────
    this.on('cheatPosition', 'Players', async (req) => {
    const targetSquare = req.data.pos
    const playerID = req.params[0].ID

    await UPDATE(Players)
    .set({ position: targetSquare })
    .where({ ID: playerID })

    return req.reply(`Player teleported to square ${targetSquare}`)
    })

    return super.init()
    }
    }

    ) 
    Added a cheat Code to change the position of the player and USIING  POST you can modify the Players position to desirable positions
  2. A screenshot showing the HTTP 409 response when calling rollDice in the wrong state

     

  3. A screenshot or OData response showing your player's position after landing on a snake or ladder

     

  4. One sentence: what happens if you call winGame on a player whose position is not 100? How does your implementation handle it?
    If you call winGame prematurely, the implementation will still successfully mark the game session as finished and declare that player the winner because there is no explicit validation check in the handler verifying that their position is exactly 100.

entry.title: Re: SAF-T Portugal - Incorrect Opening Balance calculatio...
entry.content: Based on the analysis of the accounting documents and balances, the SAF-T behavior appears to be working as designed. The SAF-T Opening Balance is being derived from the balance carryforward (the closing balance of the previous fiscal year). Since account 4310000001 had a balance of EUR 56,863.33 on 31/12/2024, this is the value used by the SAF-T as the opening balance. However, during the S/4HANA migration, an opening balance of EUR 113,726.66 (double the expected amount) was loaded into the account. The journal entries posted in January and March were then created assuming this duplicated opening balance. Later, additional adjustment postings were made so that the account ended with the correct balance. This creates the inconsistency observed today: The General Ledger and trial balance are mathematically correct because all subsequent postings were made based on an opening balance of EUR 113,726.66. The SAF-T starts from EUR 56,863.33, as it reads the balance carryforward from the previous fiscal year. When the January and March movements are applied to this opening balance, the resulting balance no longer reconciles. Therefore, the issue does not appear to be an error in the SAF-T calculation itself. Rather, there are two different opening balance references: the opening balance created during the migration process and subsequently used for accounting postings; and the balance carryforward used by the SAF-T report. The main challenge is that correcting the issue by posting additional journal entries is unlikely to solve it. Any new adjustment would modify the General Ledger balances and could also affect the final G/L accounts to which the migration balances have already been transferred. In other words, it is possible to correct the SAF-T while creating inconsistencies in the accounting records, or vice versa. For this reason, although correcting the issue "at the source" is theoretically the right approach, it is not clear how this should be done in a productive system where the migration has already been completed, accounting periods have been closed, and subsequent postings depend on the current balances. Given the complexity of the situation and the potential accounting impact, we believe the best approach is to open an SAP incident under FI-LOC-SAF-PT / Document and Reporting Compliance. SAP should be able to confirm: whether this behavior is expected in this migration scenario; whether there is a supported procedure to correct the migrated opening balance; or whether a different correction approach is recommended for productive systems. Considering the potential impact on the accounting history, we recommend obtaining SAP's official guidance before making any changes to the productive system.

entry.title: Re: MIGO Transfer Posting Against Reservation
entry.content:
In MIGO, when you perform a Transfer Posting against a Reservation, the system behavior is to display all reservation line items, regardless of whether they are already issued or still pending. This is standard SAP design because:

  • Reservation line items are not dynamically reduced from the display once issued. Even if the material has been fully withdrawn, the line remains visible in MIGO.
  • The system differentiates between issued vs. pending quantities using the “Removed Quantity” and “Requirement Quantity” fields in the reservation item.

This means the user must carefully check “Removed Quantity” and “Requirement Quantity” to avoid attempting postings against items that are already fully consumed. If your business process requires hiding fully issued items, you would need to consider a custom filter or enhancement in MIGO (e.g., via BAdI MB_MIGO_BADI) to suppress fully issued lines, or using transaction MB1B with movement type directly against the reservation, where the system prompts only for open quantities.

entry.content:
Business requirement to archive FI, CO documents that involve WBS elements in S/4HANA. The process involves archiving WBS elements using the PS_PROJECT archiving object and related CO line items via CO_TRANS. Proper mapping and handling of WBS element numbers are crucial, especially in environments like Central Finance.

My help, but no explication for subledger part : https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-additional-settings-in-financial-accounting-in-sap-s-4hana/executing-data-archiving-for-specific-fi-objects

Business requires guidance on the steps, necessary configurations, and any relevant notes or best practices to ensure consistent and complete archiving.

entry.title: Consignment Stock Return – Appropriate Customs Document for Portugal
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently working on a customer scenario and would like to understand how others handle a similar situation.

Our customer receives consignment stock from one of their suppliers. The goods are accompanied by a pro forma invoice with a value of EUR 0.00, which is used only for transportation purposes.

However, in Portugal, Customs generally only accepts pro forma invoices in specific situations (for example, when shipping samples). This has raised the following question:

What is the most appropriate document to accompany the return of consignment stock while complying with Portuguese Customs requirements?

If anyone has dealt with a similar scenario, I would appreciate your insights, especially regarding:

  • Which document do you use to accompany the return shipment?
  • Are there any best practices or specific Portuguese Customs requirements to consider?
  • How have you implemented this process in SAP?

Thank you in advance for sharing your experience and recommendations.

entry.title: 資産振替処理を実施した際の振替元資産と振替先資産のデータ状況について
entry.content:
お世話になっております。

AS01→ABUMNで資産振替を実施しました。

※振替元資産の資本化日付・初回取得日：5/31、振替日付：6/30

※取得価額：263,000円、耐用年数：5年、償却額：3,933円

処理実行後の振替元資産／振替先資産のデータ状況を確認してみると、以下の点が気になります。

【振替元資産】

・1ヶ月分の償却を反映した232,067円が貸方に計上されているが、借方に3,867円が計上。

・今年度の残りの償却額に656円が設定されており、毎月65円or66円の償却が発生する。

・2031年度まで償却が発生するスケジュールになっている。

【振替先資産】

・1ヶ月分の償却を反映した232,067円が借方に計上されているが、貸方に3,867円が計上。

・償却額が振替元資産の3,933円から3,868円になっている。

　※ユーザには償却額の計算式は「取得価額 × 耐用年数に対する償却係数(5年だと0.2) ÷ 12」である事を説明済み。

ユーザ側は以下を希望されていますが、対応可能な内容でしょうか。

・振替元資産の今年度の月65円or66円の償却額の発生をなくしたい。

・振替先資産の償却額を振替元資産と同じく3,933円にしたい。

画面資料をご確認頂いた上で、仕様のご説明をお願い致します。

entry.title: Joule for Developers
entry.content:
Joule's ABAP developer capabilities support full-stack development in ABAP Cloud. It provides real-time explanations of development objects and is fully integrated in the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, offering accurate information about open objects or marked code. Joule predicts and generates subsequent lines of code based on ABAP Doc, comments, or code being typed, taking the ABAP language version into account to suggest fitting code. It can also generate end-to-end ABAP cloud development scenarios including all required objects.

SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities is designed to support you with your day-to-day tasks when working with ABAP code, to increase your efficiency and to lower total cost of development.  In the SAP ecosystem specifically across SAP BTP (ABAP Environment) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Joule matters because it understands the proprietary context of ABAP Cloud, Core Data Services (CDS), and SAP Fiori.

You can explore and understand in detail extensively regarding the capabilities of Joule by referring to the blog - Joule Capabilities for Developers ABAP - SAP Community but this blog details on how you can get onboard and capitalise this AI developer journey.

How to get started?

This AI feature is available as a free of charge Promo until October 2026 after which it will be a Premium AI feature. 

Required Parent SKUs (The Foundation)

To activate the Joule for Developers add-on, your global account must have the underlying infrastructure provisioned. 
Customer SKU  : 8019164
Partner SKU      : 8019670

 

 

This promotional license for Joule for Developers  was sunset as of June 1,2026 . The SAP Note 3571857 for the SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and it  described the activation and enablement (commercial and technical) of the promotional license for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities. No new orders can be placed. Existing contracts will be fulfilled until end of September 2026 at the maximum.

A new commercial model for SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities was introduced, based on AI units, see SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/3740750. 


Sharing our latest blog on SAP Joule for Developers , ABAP AI capabilities, now available through a new AI Unit-based commercial license.
The blog covers:
• Licensing and commercial aspects
• Backend configuration and enablement steps and SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities in ABAP Development Tools for  Eclipse and Visual Studio Code
🔗SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities inc... - SAP Community

The capabilities of Joule for Developers are much more extensive than just basic code generation. Here is a comprehensively updated, tabularized "Feature Deep Dive" based strictly on the extracted official capabilities:

Feature

What it does

How it works technically / Examples

Joule Chat

Acts as an AI pair programmer that you can interact with using natural language for development tasks.

Uses IDE Context Awareness to read currently opened resources. It answers general ABAP questions, explains specific code snippets, and dynamically generates formatted code examples you can directly insert.

Predictive Code Completion

Automatically generates inline "ghost text" to predict and complete code as you type.

Listens to cursor position and editor context. It supports ABAP interfaces, classes, and programs. It is powered by a fine-tuned model (Star Coder 2) trained specifically on SAP internal ABAP source code.

Explain Capability

Provides detailed, semantic explanations of selected development objects or lines of source code.

Fully integrated into ADT. You can select an ABAP class, a CDS artifact, or lines of code, and Joule provides an English explanation. It also supports explaining Custom Code Migration (ATC) findings directly in-app.

Documentation Chat

Allows you to chat directly with public documentation hosted on the SAP Help Portal.

Accesses standard guides (ABAP Keyword Documentation, RAP, ABAP Data Models, ABAP Cloud) and provides referenced answers considering your current project context.

ABAP Unit Test Generation

Automatically creates and refactors ABAP Unit tests to check functional correctness.

Generates tests for public/protected/private methods. It also performs Dependency Analysis to find test-unfriendly dependencies, automatically adds Test Doubles, and helps split or refactor large test classes.

CDS Unit Test Generation

Generates automated test classes and context-specific test data for ABAP CDS entities.

Provides basic test coverage by reading the semantics of a CDS entity and using AI to generate test method names, test data, and updatable test doubles.

OData UI Service from Scratch

Generates a complete RAP BO (Business Object) and UI service from an empty package.

You prompt Joule with requirements (e.g., "Create a Travel BO with two entities"). It automatically generates the database tables, CDS views, behavior definitions, metadata extensions, and service bindings.

RAP Business Logic Prediction

Generates complex implementation logic for RAP determinations and validations.

Triggered via a quick fix inside an empty method. You provide a natural language description (e.g., what to validate, error messages, edge cases), and Joule generates the exact ABAP code for the behavior pool.

Consume Capability

Auto-generates OData requests and the corresponding ABAP code to consume remote services.

You prompt Joule (e.g., "I want to read the field ID from all campaigns"). It outputs the HTTP GET request and the specific ABAP logic required to use the OData Client Proxy.

Embedded Analytics Star Schema Generator

Uses AI to derive and create an analytical star schema based on existing CDS view entities.

Automatically generates the Cube definition (center of the star schema), dimension fields (e.g., Product, Region), and measure fields required for complex ABAP analytics.

GenAI-Driven Extensibility Assistant

Assists developers in executing various tasks related to field extensibility.

Helps find business contexts, usages, value help views, and enables business scenarios when creating custom fields for extensibility.

Custom Code Migration AI Assistant

Accelerates the migration of legacy custom code to modern, clean-core standards.

Integrates with the "Analyze Custom Code / Custom Code Migration" Fiori app to provide deep AI explanations of ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) simplification findings and legacy processing logic.


Stay tuned as many more features are soon rolling out.  

References:

  • SAP Discovery Center AI Feature - SAP BTP, ABAP environment, SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities | SAP Help Portal










References:

  • SAP Discovery Center AI Feature - SAP BTP, ABAP environment, SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Cross Docking Is Not Speed — It Is Controlled Movement
entry.content:
Cross Docking in SAP EWM: Why Synchronization Matters More Than Speed

Tags: SAP Extended Warehouse Management, SAP Transportation Management

Cross Docking in SAP EWM Why Synchronization Matters More Than Speed

Introduction

Cross Docking is often described as a warehouse process that moves inbound goods directly to outbound delivery without long-term storage. While this definition is technically correct, it can also be misleading.

During my study of warehouse execution scenarios in SAP EWM, I began asking a different question:

Is Cross Docking really about moving goods faster, or is it about synchronizing warehouse and transportation activities?

In real warehouse operations, moving a pallet from one location to another is rarely the biggest challenge. The real challenge is ensuring that the correct material reaches the correct outbound shipment at the correct time while maintaining operational stability.

From this perspective, Cross Docking becomes less about speed and more about controlled synchronization.

The Common Misunderstanding

Cross Docking is commonly associated with three benefits:

  • Reduced storage time
  • Faster material movement
  • Lower inventory handling

These benefits are real, but they only exist when warehouse execution and transportation execution remain synchronized.

Consider the following situations:

  • The outbound vehicle arrives later than planned.
  • A staging area has reached capacity.
  • Labour resources become unavailable.
  • Transportation priorities change after inbound goods have already arrived.

In these scenarios, simply moving material faster does not improve warehouse performance. Instead, uncontrolled movement may create additional handling, staging congestion, or incorrect loading sequences.

The objective therefore shifts from fast execution to controlled execution.

A Railway Station Analogy

A busy railway station provides a useful analogy.

Passengers arrive on one train and transfer to another. At first glance, this resembles Cross Docking because people move directly from one transportation flow to another.

However, when delays occur, passengers are not simply pushed onto the next platform.

Instead:

  • Waiting areas are used.
  • Platforms are reassigned.
  • Passenger flow is redirected.
  • Station operators continuously adjust movement until connections become synchronized again.

The railway station is not optimizing walking speed.

It is optimizing coordinated movement.

Warehouse Cross Docking operates in a very similar way.

How This Translates into SAP EWM

In SAP EWM, Cross Docking is supported by execution processes that connect inbound logistics with outbound requirements.

Depending on the business scenario, SAP EWM may use:

  • Warehouse Tasks (WTs) to move goods directly toward outbound staging.
  • Storage Types and Staging Areas for temporary buffering when direct movement is not possible.
  • Warehouse Process Types (WPTs) to control execution logic.
  • Door determination and staging processes to ensure the correct loading sequence.
  • Warehouse Monitor visibility to identify execution bottlenecks.

The objective is not simply to reduce storage time.

The objective is to execute warehouse movement without disrupting outbound commitments.

The Role of SAP TM

Cross Docking becomes significantly more effective when integrated with SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM).

SAP TM provides information such as:

  • Planned transportation routes
  • Vehicle arrival times (ETA)
  • Transportation Units
  • Freight Orders
  • Delivery priorities

This information allows warehouse execution to align with transportation planning before physical loading begins.

For example:

If SAP TM indicates that a vehicle will arrive two hours later than originally planned, immediately moving Cross Docking inventory to the outbound door may actually increase warehouse congestion.

Instead, SAP EWM can temporarily stage the material until transportation execution becomes synchronized again.

In this situation, synchronization creates greater operational value than immediate movement.

Temporary Staging Is Not a Failure

One misconception is that successful Cross Docking always means zero staging.

Operationally, this is not always true.

Temporary staging may be the correct business decision when:

  • Transportation schedules change.
  • Dock capacity becomes constrained.
  • Loading priorities change.
  • Labour availability decreases.
  • Multiple outbound shipments compete for the same resources.

SAP EWM provides flexibility through staging areas and storage types that allow warehouses to absorb operational variation while maintaining execution control.

Temporary staging is therefore not necessarily inefficient.

In many situations, it prevents warehouse disruption.

Visibility Drives Synchronization

Successful Cross Docking depends on continuous operational visibility.

Warehouse teams must understand:

  • Which inbound deliveries have arrived.
  • Which outbound deliveries require immediate execution.
  • Which Transportation Units are delayed.
  • Which staging locations are approaching capacity.
  • Which warehouse resources are available.

Without visibility, warehouse execution becomes reactive.

With visibility, warehouse execution becomes synchronized.

Business Value

When Cross Docking is viewed only as a speed optimization technique, warehouse teams may focus exclusively on reducing storage time.

When viewed as a synchronization strategy, different objectives emerge:

  • Reduce unnecessary handling.
  • Protect outbound service levels.
  • Improve dock utilization.
  • Align warehouse execution with transportation planning.
  • Maintain operational control during disruptions.

These outcomes provide greater long-term business value than simply moving goods faster.

Conclusion

Cross Docking should not be viewed simply as direct material movement.

Its real value lies in synchronizing warehouse execution with transportation execution while maintaining operational control.

SAP EWM provides the execution capabilities required to manage warehouse movement.

SAP TM contributes transportation planning and visibility.

Together, these systems enable coordinated execution rather than isolated movement.

In my view, the most successful Cross Docking operations are not those that move the fastest.

They are the ones that remain synchronized even when business conditions change.

Author: Rahul Kumar
Manyat Thinking

"Fast movement creates activity. Synchronized movement creates business value."

entry.title: Using Custom Logic in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Realize Insufficient Stock Quantity at Outbound Deliver
entry.content:
Problem Background

A customer has requested a feature where, due to business management requirements, they want to automatically check whether the stock quantity of materials in outbound delivery line items is sufficient when creating the delivery. Specifically, when an outbound delivery is saved, the system needs to verify if the material quantity in the corresponding sales order line items exceeds the available stock quantity. If stock is insufficient, a Warning message should be displayed indicating the material shortage. Additionally, the customer wants to see the corresponding messages when creating outbound deliveries via API.

Solution

We implemented a custom logic solution using a combination of two BAdIs to meet the requirements.

Analysis Steps

1. Identifying the Appropriate BAdI Enhancement Points

First, we needed to find suitable BAdI enhancement points to implement the stock check functionality. After analysis, we discovered that the enhancement point LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK can execute final checks before the delivery is saved. Therefore, this BAdI can be used to return Warning messages when creating outbound deliveries in the frontend APP if stock quantity is insufficient.

2. Implementing Warning Messages for Frontend APP

We implemented basic stock check logic in BAdI LE_SHP_DELIVERY_FINAL_CHECK:

    CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant    TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg    TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg    TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart    TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

    DATA:
    lv_so_qty    TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

    IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
      LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item) .
      IF ls_item-plant = lc_plant.
      " Get Order Quantity of Sales Order Items
      SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
      " Get Stock Quantity
      SELECT SINGLE MATLWRHSSTKQTYINMATLBASEUNIT
      FROM I_STOCKQUANTITYCURRENTVALUE_2( P_DisplayCurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE PRODUCT = @LS_item-MATERIAL
      INTO @LV_stock.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE. " No SO item found – skip this delivery item
      ENDIF.
      IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
        message-messagetype = 'W'. " saving of the delivery document will be warned
        message-messagetext = |Warning: The material has insufficient stock quantity|.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

3. Discovering API Scenario Limitations

However, this Warning message only displays in the frontend APP. Messages cannot be seen when creating deliveries via API. Therefore, we needed to implement an alternative solution.

4. Implementing Message Display for API Scenarios

To make the messages visible in API creation scenarios, we adopted the following approach:

Solution Details:

- Create a custom field that can be seen in the API Response
- Use BAdI LE_SHP_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE, which can modify standard and custom fields at both header and line item levels
- This BAdI is called before the delivery is saved to the database

Implementation Code:

IF documentprocessingmode = 'CREATE'.
  CONSTANTS:
    lc_plant  TYPE werks_d VALUE '1310',
    lc_vkorg  TYPE vkorg   VALUE '1310',
    lc_vtweg  TYPE vtweg   VALUE '20',
    lc_spart  TYPE spart   VALUE '00'.

  DATA:
    lv_so_qty TYPE menge_d,
    lv_stock TYPE menge_d.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 1: Must first copy complete input to output (Cloud BAdI standard practice)
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  MOVE-CORRESPONDING delivery_document_in TO delivery_document_out.
  delivery_document_items_out = delivery_document_items_in.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 2: Header organization data filter
  " Only applies to Sales Org 1310 / DC 20 / Division 00
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  *CHECK delivery_document_in-salesorganization = lc_vkorg.

  " -------------------------------------------------------
  " Step 3: Loop through delivery line items
  " -------------------------------------------------------
  LOOP AT delivery_document_items_in INTO DATA(ls_item).

    " Skip line items without reference sales order
    *CHECK ls_item-referencesddocument IS NOT INITIAL.
    *CHECK ls_item-referencesddocumentitem IS NOT INITIAL.

    " Read order quantity from sales order line item
    SELECT SINGLE orderquantity
      FROM i_salesorderitem
      WHERE salesorder     = @LS_item-referencesddocument
        AND salesorderitem = @LS_item-referencesddocumentitem
      INTO @LV_so_qty.
    " Read material stock quantity
    SELECT SINGLE matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit
      FROM i_stockquantitycurrentvalue_2( p_displaycurrency = 'CNY' )
      WHERE product = @LS_item-material
      INTO @LV_stock.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      CONTINUE. " No corresponding sales order line item found, skip
    ENDIF.

    " Compare stock quantity with sales order quantity
    IF lv_stock < lv_so_qty.
      " Update custom field
      delivery_document_out-yy1_message_dlh = 'Warning: Insufficient stock quantity for this material.'.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

5. CDS Views Used

During implementation, we used the following CDS Views to retrieve the required data:

- I_SalesOrderItem: Used to get the order quantity of sales order line items, queried by sales order number and line item number
- I_StockQuantityCurrentValue_2: Used to get the current stock quantity of materials, providing real-time stock information for materials at different storage locations, which is ideal for stock checking scenarios

6. Testing and Validation

After testing, the above code successfully meets the requirements:

- When creating outbound deliveries in the frontend APP, if stock is insufficient, a Warning message is displayed

 

- When creating outbound deliveries via API, the custom field value is updated, and the stock shortage message can be seen in the Response's custom field

 

Conclusion

By reading this article and several similar scenario blogs I've written previously, I hope you now understand how to implement stock quantity checks through custom logic when creating outbound deliveries, and how to return appropriate messages in different scenarios.

Follow this link to see more questions and blog posts related to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales - SAP Community

If you have any suggestions or ideas about this article, please feel free to share them in the comments section. I look forward to exchanging ideas with you. Also, please follow my personal account, and I hope to see you in the next blog post!
